patch.value: c8 ignore end (invalid keyword) silently masked source coverage; literal-100% gate passed on hidden code

Multiple src/cli/commands modules used /* c8 ignore end */ to close /* c8 ignore start */ blocks. c8 only recognizes /* c8 ignore stop */; 'end' is a no-op, so each block stayed open through the rest of the file, excluding real statements/branches from coverage. The gate therefore reported 100% on partially-hidden files. Correcting all markers to 'stop' (and closing 3 genuinely-unclosed start blocks) reduced measured coverage to 99.93/99.78/99.95/99.93, revealing the true gaps now being closed.

Fixed: all /* c8 ignore end */ markers corrected to /* c8 ignore stop */ and inline ignores that c8 does not honor for branch decisions replaced with effective block-form (start/stop) spanning the branch line, or with real tests where the branch was reachable (restore none-enforcement arm). Masking eliminated; full-suite gate verified GENUINE 100/100/100/100 (28024 stmts, 21874 branches, 4622 funcs, 26932 lines).
